<issue identifier="REN-74">
<title>AF-015: Implement af agent reconnect subcommand</title>
<description>
## Goal

Implement the `af agent reconnect` Cobra subcommand. It resumes an orphaned agent session by calling `POST /api/public/sessions/:id/reconnect`, returning a short confirmation that reports the current session status and how many activity events were missed while the caller was away.

```
af agent reconnect <session-id>
af agent reconnect SUP-674
af agent reconnect --cursor 2026-04-15T10:00:00Z SUP-674
af agent reconnect --last-event-id evt_abc123 SUP-674
af agent reconnect --mock SUP-674
af agent reconnect --json --mock SUP-674
```

## Implementation (on branch <issue id="e202fb75-7ba5-4c25-8da9-d4acdd1825e0">REN-74</issue>)

Files changed:

* `afcli/agent.go` — wires `newAgentReconnectCmd` into the agent parent group

Uses the `afcli/` package pattern with `func() afclient.DataSource` injection. Consumes `afclient.ReconnectSession` / `ReconnectSessionRequest` / `ReconnectSessionResponse`.

## QA Checklist

### Functional tests (run with `--mock` against MockClient)

- [ ] `af agent reconnect --mock SUP-674` prints one-line confirmation: `reconnected to SUP-674 (status: running, missed: 0 events)` and exits 0
- [ ] `af agent reconnect --mock --json SUP-674` prints valid indented JSON with `reconnected`, `sessionId`, `sessionStatus`, `missedEvents` fields and exits 0
- [ ] `af agent reconnect --mock --cursor 2026-04-15T10:00:00Z SUP-674` succeeds (cursor passed through)
- [ ] `af agent reconnect --mock --last-event-id evt_abc123 SUP-674` succeeds (last-event-id passed through)
- [ ] `af agent reconnect --mock --cursor X --last-event-id Y SUP-674` succeeds with both flags

### Argument validation

- [ ] `af agent reconnect` (no args) exits non-zero with usage error
- [ ] `af agent reconnect a b` (two args) exits non-zero with usage error
- [ ] `af agent reconnect "   "` (whitespace-only) exits non-zero with `"session id must not be empty"` error, no API call made

### Error handling

- [ ] When `ReconnectSession` returns `ErrNotFound`, error wraps it: `errors.Is(err, afclient.ErrNotFound)` succeeds
- [ ] When response has `reconnected: false`, prints `"reconnect declined for ..."` and exits non-zero

### Pluralization

- [ ] 0 events → `"missed: 0 events"`
- [ ] 1 event → `"missed: 1 event"` (singular)
- [ ] N events → `"missed: N events"` (plural)

### Build & lint

- [ ] `make build` succeeds
- [ ] `make test` passes (race detector enabled)
- [ ] `make lint` clean
- [ ] Coverage for `afcli/agent_reconnect.go` ≥ 80%

### Integration (if coordinator available)

- [ ] `af agent reconnect <real-session-id>` against a running coordinator returns expected status

## Architecture note

All commands live in `afcli/` as unexported factory functions wired via `RegisterCommands`. This follows the `afcli/agent.go`, `afcli/status.go` pattern. There is no `cmd/af/` command code.

## Out of Scope

* Other `af agent` subcommands (list, stop, status, chat)
* Streaming resumed activity events (reconnect is fire-and-forget)
* Redis / `REDIS_URL` access
* `LINEAR_API_KEY` wiring
</description>
<team name="Rensei"/>

<comment author="Mark Kropf" created-at="2026-04-21T11:55:14.776Z">
## Agent Work Complete

REN-74 complete and committed (`caebd5d`).

## Summary

**Implementation (**`afcli/agent_reconnect.go`) — note the spec referenced `cmd/af/agent.go`, but the architecture has since moved Cobra factories into `afcli/`; the new command lives there alongside its siblings (`agent_chat.go`, `agent_stop.go`, `agent_status.go`):

* `newAgentReconnectCmd(ds)` — `ExactArgs(1)`, `SilenceUsage: true`.
* Whitespace-only session IDs rejected before any RPC.
* `--cursor` / `--last-event-id` only populated on the request when `cmd.Flags().Changed(...)` is true (so empty-string flags are still forwarded deliberately, but unset flags stay nil).
* `--json` emits indented `ReconnectSessionResponse` via `json.NewEncoder(...).SetIndent("", "  ")`.
* Default human output: `reconnected to <id> (status: <s>, missed: N event[s])` with singular/plural via `pluralizeEvents`.
* `Reconnected == false` prints `reconnect declined for <id> (status: ...)` and returns a non-nil error → non-zero exit.
* Errors wrapped as `fmt.Errorf("reconnect session: %w", err)` to preserve sentinels.

**Wiring** — added `cmd.AddCommand(newAgentReconnectCmd(ds))` in `newAgentCmd` (`afcli/agent.go`).

**Tests (**`afcli/agent_reconnect_test.go`) — 11 test funcs, table-driven where applicable: help text, parent-help listing, arg-count validation, whitespace-id RPC short-circuit, mock human happy path (exact one-liner match), flag-forwarding matrix (none / cursor / last-event-id / both / explicit empty), pluralisation (0/1/many), `--json` decode, declined branch, sentinel propagation (4 sentinels), HTTP 404 propagation, plus a `pluralizeEvents` unit test. Uses a captured-request stub (`reconnectStubDataSource`) to verify the request struct without touching `MockClient`.

Note on tests: the spec's `--mock SUP-674` example came from `chat`'s permissive `ForwardPrompt` mock; `MockClient.ReconnectSession` looks up by ID and returns `ErrNotFound` for unknown values (with an existing `TestMockClientReconnectSessionNotFound` asserting that). I used `mock-001` for mock-mode tests to stay consistent with the actual mock data and avoid breaking that contract — same pattern `agent status` and `agent stop` already follow.

**Quality gates** — `make build`, `make test` (race), `make lint` (0 issues), `make vuln` (clean) all pass. Coverage for `agent_reconnect.go`: 96.9% (target ≥ 80%).

### Final Plan Status
